Author: Deo Wetton
Title: Vibe Coding Is Killing Open Source
Text:
Member-only story
Vibe Coding Is Killing Open Source
The prosperity of Vibe Coding may be built on the ruins of the open-source ecosystem.
Over the past year, Vibe Coding has almost completely rewritten the way programming works.
You no longer need to “write” code line by line yourself. Just tell Cursor, Claude, or Copilot what feature you want, which tech stack to use, and preferably that it should “feel like a certain product,” and let AI handle the rest.
Many people who previously couldn’t write code now, for the first time, have the ability to “build something.” From an individual perspective, this is almost a golden age of software development.
But there is an overlooked premise here: AI does not create code out of thin air. It calls upon and assembles the accumulated wisdom of humanity. When you say, “Help me build a website,” AI is quietly referencing the logic and structure accumulated across countless open-source projects on GitHub.
The core capability of Vibe Coding is built precisely on learning from and recombining these open-source codebases.
Recently, a research team from Central European University and the Kiel Institute for the World Economy published a paper titled “Vibe Coding Kills Open Source” (https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.15494v1), revealing the hidden crisis behind the prosperity of Vibe…
